english: Shack Red wine bottle 25 fl oz

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 35748005 covers wine broadly; no red-wine-specific code available.

serving.natural_language_portion: One 25 fl oz (approximately 739 mL) bottle of Shack Red wine, as served at Shake Shack Singapore locations, equivalent to approximately five standard 150 mL wine pours.

nutrient_source.title: Wine, table, red — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174835)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are taken from USDA SR Legacy 'Wine, table, red' (FDC ID 174835) per 100 g. The Shake Shack 'Shack Red' is a proprietary house red wine served in a 25 fl oz bottle; no independent laboratory analysis is publicly available. USDA SR Legacy generic red table wine is the closest approved-source match. Values are per 100 g of wine (density ~1.0 g/mL assumed for nutrient scaling).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of alcoholic beverages — red wine analogous estimate; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Red wine has a very low GI (approximately 15) due to minimal residual carbohydrate and the inhibitory effect of ethanol on gastric emptying and hepatic glucose output. GI of 15 is drawn from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for red wine and corroborated by Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (15 × 19.3) / 100 ≈ 2.9, where carbohydrate per 739 g serving = 2.61 × 7.39 ≈ 19.3 g.

hypertension.guidance: Regular alcohol consumption raises blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit red wine to no more than one standard drink per day and consult their physician before consuming a 25 fl oz bottle in a single sitting.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Ethanol-induced hypoglycaemia risk and caloric density
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although red wine has a low glycemic index (~15), the ethanol content (~10–13% ABV) can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms and impair hepatic glucose release; people with Type 2 Diabetes should consume alcohol only with food and monitor blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While moderate red wine consumption has been associated with polyphenol benefits, a 25 fl oz serving far exceeds recommended limits and the net cardiovascular risk from excess alcohol outweighs any potential benefit; individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict intake to guideline-recommended amounts.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Shack Red (25 fl oz) is a proprietary house red wine offered by Shake Shack at its Singapore locations, sold as a 25 fluid ounce (approximately 739 mL) bottle. The product is a commercially sourced red table wine, fermented from red grapes, and is typical of medium-bodied American or international house red wines with notes of dark fruit, mild tannins, and a dry finish. As a red wine, it contains approximately 85 kcal per 100 g, with the caloric content derived almost entirely from ethanol (approximately 10–13% ABV) rather than carbohydrates (2.6 g per 100 g) or fat (effectively zero). The wine contains trace protein (0.07 g per 100 g), negligible dietary fibre, and low sodium (5 mg per 100 g). It provides modest amounts of potassium (127 mg per 100 g) and iron (0.46 mg per 100 g). The glycemic index is low (~15) because residual sugars are minimal and ethanol suppresses hepatic glucose output. Clinically, the primary concerns are the substantial alcohol load in a 25 fl oz serving (equivalent to approximately five standard drinks), which poses risks for hypertension, hypoglycaemia in diabetic individuals, and cardiovascular strain when consumed in excess. The product is not halal and is not suitable for individuals who abstain from alcohol. It is gluten-free and dairy-free. In Singapore, this item is listed on field survey under Shake Shack at 89 Neil Road and One Fullerton, priced at SGD 48.70.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Shake Shack Shack Red wine?
What is the alcohol content of Shack Red?
Shake Shack red wine Singapore calories
Is red wine good for diabetics?
Shake Shack wine bottle Singapore price

confidence_reason: The Shake Shack 'Shack Red' is a proprietary house wine with no publicly available independent laboratory analysis. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy generic red table wine (FDC ID 174835), which is the closest approved-source match. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium because the exact grape variety, vintage, and ABV of the Shack Red are not publicly disclosed, introducing uncertainty in the nutrient profile.
notes: 1. Fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for red table wine; fat is naturally zero in wine by product composition. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all confirmed zero per the same source. 2. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy; wine contains no dietary fibre. 3. Cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 4. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg are confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 5. The serving size of 739 g is based on 25 fl oz × 29.5735 mL/fl oz ≈ 739 mL, with wine density approximated at 1.0 g/mL. 6. Glycemic load per serving = (15 × 19.3) / 100 ≈ 2.9, where 19.3 g carbohydrate = 2.61 g/100g × 739 g / 100. 7. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Meat and alternatives' (input) to 'Beverages' as this is an alcoholic beverage. 8. The cuisine tag 'Burgers' from the input is retained in aliases but the canonical cuisine is reclassified to 'Beverages'. 9. Vegan flag is false because many red wines use animal-derived fining agents (e.g., isinglass, casein, egg albumin); the Shack Red fining process is not publicly disclosed. 10. No -1 sentinel values remain in per_100g; all fields are either confirmed numeric values or confirmed zero from the USDA source.

Verified GI is numeric >0 with approved source. Confirmed no -1 sentinel values remain undocumented.
